title: Podman is creating broken filesystems between layers when symlinks overwrite existing symlinks on the same layer
body:
### Issue Description

I am linking this issue directly to the issue that @mmerickel created over on the Buildah repository as the same issue exists and requires attention as it is creating broken images:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5861

### Steps to reproduce the issue

See https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5861

### Describe the results you received

symlinks are not updated correctly.

### Describe the results you expected

Symlinks are updated correctly.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Copying from my comment here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5861#issuecomment-2518812779, I was able to reduce the test case down to to the following:

```
FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN touch file1.ext && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file1.ext && cp -pr file1.ext file1.alt
RUN ln -s file1.ext file2.sym && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym
RUN ls -lah file*
RUN ln -s file1.alt file2.sym.new && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym.new && mv file2.sym.new file2.sym && ls -lah file*
RUN ls -lah file*
```

With output:

```
% podman build -t testing -f Dockerfile.simple
STEP 1/6: FROM ubuntu:latest
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:8bb55f0677778c3027fcc4253dc452bc9c22de989a696391e739fb1cdbbdb4c2
Copying config sha256:20377134ad8875ad73c4a4f12b5d8e28c8665da80756c2afbb47d1f730bf2e5e
Writing manifest to image destination
STEP 2/6: RUN touch file1.ext && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file1.ext && cp -pr file1.ext file1.alt
--> 8360f2dc5e6f
STEP 3/6: RUN ln -s file1.ext file2.sym && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym
--> a9806d70ad90
STEP 4/6: RUN ls -lah file*
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.alt
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.ext
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 9 Apr  7  1988 file2.sym -> file1.ext
--> 1cfe448ef2ba
STEP 5/6: RUN ln -s file1.alt file2.sym.new && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym.new && mv file2.sym.new file2.sym && ls -lah file*
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.alt
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.ext
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 9 Apr  7  1988 file2.sym -> file1.alt <--- SYMLINK POINTS AT EXPECTED FILE
--> 3a1720b0ea6a
STEP 6/6: RUN ls -lah file*
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.alt
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 Apr  7  1988 file1.ext
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 9 Apr  7  1988 file2.sym -> file1.ext <-- SYMLINK POINTS AT WRONG FILE
COMMIT testing
--> 0215e1b91ea8
Successfully tagged localhost/testing:latest
0215e1b91ea8ba91e41be288aeb8375c9b36dfce75a2addc8cd592cab1631ec1
```

So if the symlink metadata and the path length do not change (`file1.ext` and `file1.alt` are both the same string length) then Podman/Buildah fail to notice the change in the symlink and produce a broken image.

```
% podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.2.5
API Version:  5.2.5
Go Version:   go1.23.2
Git Commit:   10c5aa720d59480bc7edad347c1f5d5b75d4424f
Built:        Wed Oct 23 11:49:37 2024
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.2.5
API Version:  5.2.5
Go Version:   go1.22.7
Built:        Thu Oct 17 18:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

Does not work

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.1
API Version:  4.4.1
Go Version:   go1.19.13
Built:        Thu Oct 24 14:21:08 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

which is running on RHEL 9.2 does work. So a regression somewhere? Or linux kernel differences?
Let's keep the conversation on the buildah issue
